root_text.sn46.53:1.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
root_text.sn46.53:2.2: ‘yasmiṁ, āvuso, samaye līnaṁ cittaṁ hoti, katamesaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bojjhaṅgānaṁ akālo bhāvanāya, katamesaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bojjhaṅgānaṁ kālo bhāvanāya?
root_text.sn46.53:2.3: Yasmiṁ panāvuso, samaye uddhataṁ cittaṁ hoti, katamesaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bojjhaṅgānaṁ akālo bhāvanāya, katamesaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bojjhaṅgānaṁ kālo bhāvanāyā’ti?
root_text.sn46.53:2.4: Evaṁ puṭṭhā, bhikkhave, aññatitthiyā paribbājakā na ceva sampāyissanti, uttariñca vighātaṁ āpajjissanti.
root_text.sn46.53:3.1: Nāhaṁ taṁ, bhikkhave, passāmi sadevake loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya yo imesaṁ pañhānaṁ veyyākaraṇena cittaṁ ārādheyya, aññatra tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā ito vā pana sutvā.
root_text.sn46.53:4.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye līnaṁ cittaṁ hoti, akālo tasmiṁ samaye passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:4.3: Līnaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi dussamuṭṭhāpayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:5.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso parittaṁ aggiṁ ujjāletukāmo assa.
root_text.sn46.53:5.2: So tattha allāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, allāni ca gomayāni pakkhipeyya, allāni ca kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya, udakavātañca dadeyya, paṁsukena ca okireyya;
root_text.sn46.53:5.3: bhabbo nu kho so puriso parittaṁ aggiṁ ujjāletun”ti?
root_text.sn46.53:6.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye līnaṁ cittaṁ hoti, akālo tasmiṁ samaye passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:6.3: Līnaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi dussamuṭṭhāpayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:7.1: Yasmiñca kho, bhikkhave, samaye līnaṁ cittaṁ hoti, kālo tasmiṁ samaye dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:7.3: Līnaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi susamuṭṭhāpayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:8.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso parittaṁ aggiṁ ujjāletukāmo assa.
root_text.sn46.53:8.2: So tattha sukkhāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni gomayāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya, mukhavātañca dadeyya, na ca paṁsukena okireyya;
root_text.sn46.53:8.3: bhabbo nu kho so puriso parittaṁ aggiṁ ujjāletun”ti?
root_text.sn46.53:9.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye līnaṁ cittaṁ hoti, kālo tasmiṁ samaye dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:9.3: Līnaṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi susamuṭṭhāpayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:10.1: Yasmiṁ, bhikkhave, samaye uddhataṁ cittaṁ hoti, akālo tasmiṁ samaye dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:10.3: Uddhataṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi duvūpasamayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:11.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso mahantaṁ aggikkhandhaṁ nibbāpetukāmo assa.
root_text.sn46.53:11.2: So tattha sukkhāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni ca gomayāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni ca kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya, mukhavātañca dadeyya, na ca paṁsukena okireyya;
root_text.sn46.53:11.3: bhabbo nu kho so puriso mahantaṁ aggikkhandhaṁ nibbāpetun”ti?
root_text.sn46.53:12.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye uddhataṁ cittaṁ hoti, akālo tasmiṁ samaye dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo vīriyasambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, akālo pītisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:12.3: Uddhataṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi duvūpasamayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:13.1: Yasmiñca kho, bhikkhave, samaye uddhataṁ cittaṁ hoti, kālo tasmiṁ samaye passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:13.3: Uddhataṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi suvūpasamayaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn46.53:14.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso mahantaṁ aggikkhandhaṁ nibbāpetukāmo assa.
root_text.sn46.53:14.2: So tattha allāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, allāni ca gomayāni pakkhipeyya, allāni ca kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya, udakavātañca dadeyya, paṁsukena ca okireyya;
root_text.sn46.53:14.3: bhabbo nu kho so puriso mahantaṁ aggikkhandhaṁ nibbāpetun”ti?
root_text.sn46.53:15.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yasmiṁ samaye uddhataṁ cittaṁ hoti, kālo tasmiṁ samaye passaddhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo samādhisambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya, kālo upekkhāsambojjhaṅgassa bhāvanāya.
root_text.sn46.53:15.3: Uddhataṁ, bhikkhave, cittaṁ taṁ etehi dhammehi suvūpasamayaṁ hoti.

translation_text.sn46.53:1.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.sn46.53:2.1: “Mendicants, when wanderers of other religions say this, you should say to them:
translation_text.sn46.53:2.2: ‘Reverends, which awakening factors should not be developed when the mind is sluggish? And which awakening factors should be developed at that time?
translation_text.sn46.53:2.3: Which awakening factors should not be developed when the mind is restless? And which awakening factors should be developed at that time?’
translation_text.sn46.53:2.4: Questioned like this, the wanderers of other religions would be stumped, and, in addition, would get frustrated.
translation_text.sn46.53:2.6: Because they’re out of their element.
translation_text.sn46.53:3.1: I don’t see anyone in this world—with its gods, Māras, and Divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, its gods and humans—who could provide a satisfying answer to these questions except for the Realized One or his disciple or someone who has heard it from them.
translation_text.sn46.53:4.1: When the mind is sluggish, it’s the wrong time to develop the awakening factors of tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.53:4.3: Because it’s hard to stimulate a sluggish mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:5.1: Suppose someone wanted to make a small fire flare up.
translation_text.sn46.53:5.2: If they toss wet grass, cow-dung, and timber on it, spray it with water, and scatter dirt on it,
translation_text.sn46.53:5.3: could they make it flare up?”
translation_text.sn46.53:6.1: “In the same way, when the mind is sluggish, it’s the wrong time to develop the awakening factors of tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.53:6.3: Because it’s hard to stimulate a sluggish mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:7.1: When the mind is sluggish, it’s the right time to develop the awakening factors of investigation of principles, energy, and rapture.
translation_text.sn46.53:7.3: Because it’s easy to stimulate a sluggish mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:8.1: Suppose someone wanted to make a small fire flare up.
translation_text.sn46.53:8.2: If they toss dry grass, cow-dung, and timber on it, blow on it, and don’t scatter dirt on it,
translation_text.sn46.53:8.3: could they make it flare up?”
translation_text.sn46.53:9.1: “In the same way, when the mind is sluggish, it’s the right time to develop the awakening factors of investigation of principles, energy, and rapture.
translation_text.sn46.53:9.3: Because it’s easy to stimulate a sluggish mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:10.1: When the mind is restless, it’s the wrong time to develop the awakening factors of investigation of principles, energy, and rapture.
translation_text.sn46.53:10.3: Because it’s hard to settle a restless mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:11.1: Suppose someone wanted to extinguish a great mass of fire.
translation_text.sn46.53:11.2: If they toss dry grass, cow-dung, and timber on it, blow on it, and don’t scatter dirt on it,
translation_text.sn46.53:12.1: “In the same way, when the mind is restless, it’s the wrong time to develop the awakening factors of investigation of principles, energy, and rapture.
translation_text.sn46.53:12.3: Because it’s hard to settle a restless mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:13.1: When the mind is restless, it’s the right time to develop the awakening factors of tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.53:13.3: Because it’s easy to settle a restless mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:14.1: Suppose someone wanted to extinguish a great mass of fire.
translation_text.sn46.53:14.2: If they toss wet grass, cow-dung, and timber on it, spray it with water, and scatter dirt on it,
translation_text.sn46.53:15.1: “In the same way, when the mind is restless, it’s the right time to develop the awakening factors of tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.sn46.53:15.3: Because it’s easy to settle a restless mind with these things.
translation_text.sn46.53:15.4: But mindfulness is always useful, I say.”
